definition:
1. to bear fruit  or bear as fruit , (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
2. Mid. to get fruit for oneself , i. e., 
3. to reap crops from  land, (Herdotus Historicus): metaphorically to exhaust  or drain , τὴν Ἑλλάδα (Aristophanes Comicus) 
4. to enjoy the interest  of money, (Demosthenes Orator); so in perfect passive, τὸ ἐργαστήριον κεκαρπωμένος enjoying the profits of  the shop, (Demosthenes Orator):—;absolute to make profit , (Aristophanes Comicus) 
5. to reap the fruits of, enjoy the free use of , (Thucydides), etc.: —;then, 
6. simply, to enjoy , (Sophocles Tragicus), etc.:—; sometimes in bad sense, καρποῦσθαι τὴν ἁμαρτίαν (Aeschulus Tragicus); πένθη (Euripides) (ML)
